title: Galle 3-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Galle 3-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 3-day trip to Galle, Sri Lanka: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Galle Fort ramparts and museum and National Maritime Museum, Galle Fort. Prices from $1580 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 3 days discovering Galle, Sri Lanka — Tropical coastal climate with warm temperatures year-round (highs 29-31°C); the southwest monsoon (May-September) brings the heaviest rain and rougher surf, while December-March is drier with calmer seas ideal for the fort ramparts and nearby beaches. December to March for the driest, sunniest weather on the south coast (highs 29-31°C); the southwest monsoon (May-September) brings rougher seas and less reliable beach weather. This itinerary blends Galle Fort ramparts and museum, National Maritime Museum, Galle Fort, Galle Fort ramparts walk with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
2 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Galle Fort ramparts and museum, National Maritime Museum, Galle Fort, Galle Fort ramparts walk
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Galle
itinerary.description: Land at Bandaranaike International Airport (nearest gateway, ~120km) (CMB) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Galle Fort ramparts and museum — 17th-century Dutch colonial fort, UNESCO World Heritage Site. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Galle: National Maritime Museum, Galle Fort & Galle Lighthouse area
itinerary.description: Morning visit to National Maritime Museum, Galle Fort — Maritime history and shipwreck artifacts inside the fort walls. In the afternoon, continue to Galle Lighthouse area — Sri Lanka's oldest lighthouse at the fort's southeastern point. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Japanese Peace Pagoda, Rumassala or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to CMB for your departure flight.

travelTips: Walk the fort ramparts at sunset for the best light and cooler temperatures — Flag Rock is the classic spot.
Take the scenic coastal train from Colombo to Galle for ocean views most of the route.
Combine Galle with a Mirissa whale-watching trip (Nov-Apr) — boats depart early morning for the best sightings.
Book fort-wall boutique hotels well ahead in peak season (Dec-Mar); rooms inside the walls are limited.
Unawatuna and Jungle Beach are a short, cheap tuk-tuk ride from the fort — no need to stay directly on the beach.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Light, breathable clothing for tropical heat and humidity
A modest cover-up for mosque/temple visits inside the fort
Reef-safe sunscreen for nearby snorkeling beaches
Comfortable sandals for uneven rampart stones and cobbled lanes
thingsToDo: Whale watching, Mirissa (day trip from Galle)
Whale and dolphin watching at Mirissa
Surfing lessons at nearby Weligama or Midigama
Stand-up paddleboarding on calm fort-side waters
thingsToAvoid: Swimming at unmarked or unsupervised beaches during monsoon swell (May-Sep) — rip currents are a real risk.
Overpriced "gem" and "sapphire" sales pitches from unlicensed dealers.
Driving/walking on the ramparts edge in wet weather — the stones get slippery.
Booking whale-watching tours with operators that crowd or chase whales — choose responsible-tourism certified operators.
Assuming ATMs are widely available inside the fort — withdraw cash before entering the old town.
topRestaurants: Church Street Social — Sri Lankan Fusion ($$$)
Poonie's Kitchen — Sri Lankan Home Cooking ($)
Mama's Galle Fort Roof Cafe — Sri Lankan & Continental, rooftop ($$)
The Fort Printers restaurant — Fine-dining Sri Lankan ($$$$)
shoppingAreas: Galle Fort gem and jewelry stores
hiddenGems: Galle Fort's quieter southern ramparts at dawn
adventureActivities: Whale and dolphin watching at Mirissa
Surfing lessons at nearby Weligama or Midigama
Stand-up paddleboarding on calm fort-side waters
luxuryExperiences: Boutique stay at The Fort Printers or Amangalla
Private sunset rampart walk with a historian guide
Fine dining at Church Street Social
honeymoonExperiences: Candlelight dinner in a restored Dutch courtyard
Private beach picnic at Jungle Beach
Boutique fort hotel stay with a private plunge pool
budgetExperiences: Free rampart walk around the entire fort
Street food at the Galle bus-stand food stalls
Self-guided colonial architecture walk through the fort lanes

festivalCalendar.name: Vesak Festival
festivalCalendar.timing: May (full moon), lantern displays across town

festivalCalendar.name: Galle Fort Christmas & New Year celebrations

localEtiquette: Dress modestly when entering the Meeran Mosque or Sudharmalaya Temple inside the Fort.
Ask permission before photographing residents inside their private courtyards along the Fort's narrow lanes.
Respect quiet hours in residential parts of the Fort, especially in the evenings.
Support Fort artisans and boutique shops directly rather than only mass-produced souvenir stalls.
localTransportGuide: Galle Fort itself is entirely walkable and largely car-restricted inside the walls; tuk-tuks (best booked via the PickMe app for fair pricing) cover trips to Unawatuna, Jungle Beach and the bus/train station. The scenic coastal train line connects Galle to Colombo and onward to Mirissa/Matara.
bestTimeToVisit: December to March for the driest, sunniest weather on the south coast (highs 29-31°C); the southwest monsoon (May-September) brings rougher seas and less reliable beach weather.
weather: Tropical coastal climate with warm temperatures year-round (highs 29-31°C); the southwest monsoon (May-September) brings the heaviest rain and rougher surf, while December-March is drier with calmer seas ideal for the fort ramparts and nearby beaches.
visaInformation: Most foreign nationals, including Indian passport holders, need an Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) approved online before arrival into Sri Lanka via Colombo, then travel overland or by rail to Galle.
averageFlightTime: ~10.4h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Galle?
faqs.answer: Most foreign nationals, including Indian passport holders, need an Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) approved online before arrival into Sri Lanka via Colombo, then travel overland or by rail to Galle.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Galle?
faqs.answer: December to March for the driest, sunniest weather on the south coast (highs 29-31°C); the southwest monsoon (May-September) brings rougher seas and less reliable beach weather. Tropical coastal climate with warm temperatures year-round (highs 29-31°C); the southwest monsoon (May-September) brings the heaviest rain and rougher surf, while December-March is drier with calmer seas ideal for the fort ramparts and nearby beaches.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Galle safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Galle is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Galle?
faqs.answer: The local currency is LKR. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Galle is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunset at Flag Rock together experience.

reviews.author: Isabella Fernandez
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to National Maritime Museum, Galle Fort and Flag Rock. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Noah Sharma
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Our trip to Galle was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Flag Rock was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Olivia Smith
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Our trip to Galle was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Japanese Peace Pagoda, Rumassala was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Fatima Patel
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Galle Fort ramparts walk alone was worth the trip, but combined with Galle Fort lanes this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Arjun Ivanov
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Flag Rock in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Lucas Tanaka
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Galle Fort cricket ground and Galle Fort ramparts and museum. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Elena Wang
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Japanese Peace Pagoda, Rumassala and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
